paragraphs.en: U.S. customers historically tipped people they assumed were earning most of their income via tips, such as restaurant servers earning less than the minimum wage. In the early 2010s, a wide range of businesses started processing purchases with iPads and other digital payment systems. These systems often prompted customers to tip for services that were not previously tipped.
paragraphs.zh: 历史上，美国顾客向他们认为通过小费赚取大部分收入的人提供小费，例如收入低于最低工资的餐馆服务员。 2010 年代初，许多企业开始使用 iPad 和其他数字支付系统处理购买行为。这些系统经常提示顾客为以前没有给过小费的服务支付小费。

paragraphs.en: Today’s tip requests are often not connected to the salary and service norms that used to determine when and how people tip. Customers in the past nearly always paid tips after receiving a service, such as at the conclusion of a restaurant meal, after getting a haircut or once a pizza was delivered. That timing could reward high-quality service and give workers an incentive to provide it.
paragraphs.zh: 如今的小费请求通常与用于确定人们何时以及如何给小费的工资和服务规范无关。过去，顾客几乎总是在接受服务后支付小费，例如在餐厅用餐结束时、理发后或披萨送达后。这个时机可以奖励高质量的服务，并激励员工提供服务。

paragraphs.en: It’s becoming more common for tips to be requested beforehand. And new tipping technology may even automatically add tips.
paragraphs.zh: 提前索取小费的情况变得越来越普遍。新的小费技术甚至可以自动添加小费。

paragraphs.en: The prevalence of digital payment devices has made it easier to ask customers for a tip.
paragraphs.zh: 数字支付设备的普及使得向顾客索要小费变得更加容易。

paragraphs.en: That helps explain why tip requests are creeping into new kinds of services. Customers now routinely see menus of suggested default options — often well above 20% of what they owe.
paragraphs.zh: 这有助于解释为什么小费请求正在渗透到新型服务中。现在，客户经常会看到建议的默认选项菜单——通常远高于他们所欠金额的 20%。

paragraphs.en: The amounts have risen from 10% or less in the 1950s to 15% around the year 2000 to 20% or higher today. This increase is sometimes called tipflation — the expectation of ever-higher tip amounts.
paragraphs.zh: 这一数字已从 2000 年左右的 1950 年代的 10% 或更少上升到 15%，再到今天的 20% 或更高。这种增加有时被称为小费膨胀——小费金额越来越高的预期。

paragraphs.en: Tipping has always been a vital source of income for workers in historically tipped services, like restaurants, where the tipped minimum wage can be as low as US $2.13 an hour.
paragraphs.zh: 对于历史上需要小费的服务业（例如餐馆）的工人来说，小费一直是重要的收入来源，这些服务业的小费最低工资可低至每小时 2.13 美元。

paragraphs.en: Tip creep and tipflation are now further supplementing the income of many low-wage service workers.
paragraphs.zh: 小费攀升和小费膨胀现在进一步补充了许多低薪服务业工人的收入。

paragraphs.en: Notably, tipping primarily benefits some of these workers, such as waiters, but not others, such as cooks and dishwashers. To ensure that all employees were paid fair wages, some restaurants banned tipping and increased prices, but this movement towards no-tipping services has largely fizzled out.
paragraphs.zh: 值得注意的是，小费主要有利于其中一些工人，例如服务员，但不利于其他工人，例如厨师和洗碗工。为了确保所有员工得到公平的工资，一些餐馆禁止给小费并提高价格，但这种不给小费服务的运动基本上以失败告终。

paragraphs.en: So to increase employee wages without raising prices, more employers are succumbing to temptations of tip creep and tipflation. However, many customers are frustrated because they feel they are being asked for too high of a tip too often. And, as our research emphasizes, tipping now seems to be more coercive, less generous, and often completely dissociated from service quality.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，为了在不提高物价的情况下提高员工工资，越来越多的雇主屈服于小费攀升和小费膨胀的诱惑。然而，许多顾客感到沮丧，因为他们觉得自己经常被要求支付过高的小费。而且，正如我们的研究所强调的那样，现在的小费似乎更具强制性，不那么慷慨，而且往往与服务质量完全无关。

questions.text: According to Paragraph 1, the practice of tipping in the U.S. ____
options: was regarded as a sign of generosity.
was a way of rewarding diligence.

questions.text: Compared with tips in the past, today’s tips ____
options: are less often requested in advance.
have less to do with service quality.

questions.text: Tip requests are creeping into new kinds of services as a result of ____
The movement toward no-tipping services was intended to ____
It can be learned from the last paragraph that tipping ____
options: is becoming a burden for customers.
reflects the need to reduce prices.

paragraphs.en: When it was established, the National Health Service (NHS) was visionary: offering high- quality, timely care to meet the dominant needs of the population it served. Nearly 75 years on, with the UK facing very different health challenges, it is clear that the model is out of date.
paragraphs.zh: 国家医疗服务体系 (NHS) 成立之初就抱有远见：提供高质量、及时的护理，以满足其所服务人群的主要需求。近 75 年过去了，英国面临着截然不同的健康挑战，显然该模型已经过时了。

paragraphs.en: From life expectancy to cancer and infant mortality rates, we are lagging behind many of our peers. With more than 6.8 million on waitlists, healthcare is becoming increasingly inaccessible for those who cannot opt to pay for private treatment; and the cost of providing healthcare is increasingly squeezing our investment in other public services. As demand for healthcare continues to grow, pressures on the workforce — which is already near breaking point — will only become more acute.
paragraphs.zh: 从预期寿命到癌症和婴儿死亡率，我们落后于许多同龄人。等候名单上有超过 680 万人，对于那些无法选择支付私人治疗费用的人来说，医疗保健变得越来越困难；提供医疗保健的成本日益挤压我们对其他公共服务的投资。随着医疗保健需求持续增长，劳动力面临的压力（已经接近临界点）只会变得更加严峻。

paragraphs.en: Many of the answers to the crisis in health and care are well rehearsed. We need to be much better at reducing and diverting demand on health services, rather than simply managing it. Much more needs to be invested in communities and primary care to reduce our reliance on hospitals. And capacity in social care needs to be greater, to support the growing number of people living with long-term conditions.
paragraphs.zh: 针对健康和护理危机的许多答案都是经过精心设计的。我们需要更好地减少和转移对卫生服务的需求，而不是简单地对其进行管理。需要对社区和初级保健进行更多投资，以减少我们对医院的依赖。社会护理能力需要更大，以支持越来越多的长期患有疾病的人。

paragraphs.en: Yet despite two decades of strategies and a number of major health reforms, we have failed to make meaningful progress on any of these aims. That is why the Reform think tank is launching a new programme of work entitled “Reimagining health”, supported by ten former health ministers. Together, we are calling for a much more open and honest conversation about the future of health in the UK, and an “urgent rethink” of the hospital-centric model we retain.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，尽管制定了二十年的战略并进行了一系列重大的卫生改革，我们仍未能在这些目标上取得有意义的进展。这就是为什么改革智囊团正在启动一项题为“重新构想健康”的新工作计划，并得到了十位前卫生部长的支持。我们共同呼吁就英国健康的未来进行更加公开和诚实的对话，并“紧急重新思考”我们保留的以医院为中心的模式。

paragraphs.en: This must begin with the question of how we maximise the health of the nation, rather than “fix” the NHS. It is estimated, for example, that healthcare accounts for only about 20% of health outcomes. Much more important are the places we live, work and socialise — yet there is no clear cross-government strategy for improving these social determinants of health.
paragraphs.zh: 这必须从如何最大限度地提高国民健康水平开始，而不是“修复”国民医疗服务体系。例如，据估计，医疗保健仅占健康结果的 20% 左右。更重要的是我们生活、工作和社交的地方——但目前还没有明确的跨政府战略来改善这些健康的社会决定因素。

paragraphs.en: Worse, when policies like the national obesity strategy are scrapped, taxpayers are left with the hefty price tag of treating the illnesses, like diabetes, that result.
paragraphs.zh: 更糟糕的是，当国家肥胖战略等政策被废除时，纳税人将承担治疗糖尿病等疾病的高昂费用。

paragraphs.en: Reform wants to ask how power and resources should be distributed in our health system.
paragraphs.zh: 改革想问的是我们的卫生系统中的权力和资源应该如何分配。

paragraphs.en: What health functions should remain at the centre, and what should be given to local leaders, often responsible for services that create health, and with a much better understanding of the needs of their populations?
paragraphs.zh: 哪些卫生职能应保留在中心位置，哪些应交给地方领导人，他们通常负责创造健康的服务，并能更好地了解人民的需求？

questions.text: According to the first two paragraphs, the NHS ____
options: can barely retain its current employees.
is rivalled by private medical services.

questions.text: One answer to the crisis in health and care is to ____
options: lighten the burden on social care.

questions.text: “Reimagining health” is aimed to ____
To maximise the nation’s health, the author suggests ____
options: paying due attention to social factors.

questions.text: It can be inferred that local leaders should ____
options: develop a stronger sense of responsibility.
play a bigger role in the health system.

paragraphs.en: Heat action plans, or HAPs, have been proliferating in India in the past few years. In general, an HAP spells out when and how officials should issue heat warnings and alert hospitals and other institutions. Nagpur’s plan, for instance, calls for hospitals to set aside “cold wards” in the summer for treating heatstroke patients, and advises builders to give construction laborers a break from work on very hot days.
paragraphs.zh: 过去几年，印度的高温行动计划（HAP）不断增多。一般来说，HAP 详细说明了官员应何时以及如何发布高温警报并向医院和其他机构发出警报。 Nagpur’s plan, for instance, calls for hospitals to set aside “cold wards” in the summer for treating heatstroke patients, and advises builders to give construction laborers a break from work on very hot days.

paragraphs.en: But implementation of existing HAPs has been uneven, according to a report from the center for Policy Research. Many lack adequate funding, it found. And their triggering thresholds often are not customized to the local climate. In some areas, high daytime temperatures alone might serve as an adequate trigger for alerts. But in other places, nighttime temperatures or humidity might be as important a gauge of risk as daytime highs.
paragraphs.zh: 但根据政策研究中心的一份报告，现有 HAP 的实施情况并不均衡。报告发现，许多人缺乏足够的资金。而且它们的触发阈值通常不是根据当地气候定制的。在某些地区，仅白天高温就足以触发警报。但在其他地方，夜间温度或湿度可能与白天的最高温度一样重要。

paragraphs.en: Mumbai’s April heat stroke deaths highlighted the need for more nuanced and localized warnings, researchers say. That day’s high temperature of roughly 36°C was 1°C shy of the heat wave alert threshold for coastal cities set by national meteorological authorities. But the effects of the heat were amplified by humidity — an often neglected factor in heat alert systems — and the lack of shade at the late-morning outdoor ceremony.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人员表示，孟买四月份的中暑死亡事件凸显了需要采取更细致、更局部的警告。当日最高气温约36℃，较国家气象部门设定的沿海城市热浪警报阈值低1℃。但湿度（高温警报系统中经常被忽视的一个因素）以及上午晚些时候户外仪式上缺乏遮荫的情况放大了高温的影响。

paragraphs.en: To help improve HAPs, urban planner Kotharkar’s team is working on a model plan that outlines best practices and could be adapted to local conditions. Among other things, she says, all cities should create a vulnerability map to help focus responses on the populations most at risk.
paragraphs.zh: 为了帮助改善 HAP，城市规划师 Kotharkar 的团队正在制定一个模型计划，该计划概述了最佳实践，并且可以根据当地情况进行调整。她说，除其他外，所有城市都应该创建一个脆弱性地图，以帮助将应对措施集中在最危险的人群上。

paragraphs.en: Such mapping doesn’t need to be complex, Kotharkar says. “A useful map can be created by looking at even a few key parameters.” For example, neighborhoods with a large elderly population or informal dwellings that cope poorly with heat could get special warnings or be bolstered with cooling centers. The Nagpur project has already created a risk and vulnerability map, which enabled Kotharkar to tell officials which neighborhoods to focus on in the event of a heat wave this summer.
paragraphs.zh: 科塔卡说，这种映射不需要很复杂。 “甚至可以通过查看几个关键参数来创建有用的地图。”例如，老年人口较多的社区或应对高温能力较差的非正式住宅可能会收到特别警告或得到降温中心的支持。那格浦尔项目已经创建了风险和脆弱性地图，这使得科塔卡能够告诉官员在今年夏天发生热浪时应该关注哪些社区。

paragraphs.en: HAPs shouldn’t just include short-term emergency responses, researchers say, but also recommend medium- to long-term measures that could make communities cooler. In Nagpur, for example, Kotharkar’s team has been able to advise city officials about where to plant trees to provide shade. HAPs could also guide efforts to retrofit homes or modify building regulations. “Reducing deaths in an emergency is a good target to have, but it’s the lowest target,” says Climate researcher Chandni Singh.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人员表示，HAP 不应只包括短期应急响应，还应建议采取中长期措施，使社区降温。例如，在那格浦尔，科塔卡的团队已经能够向市政府官员提供有关在哪里种植树木以提供遮荫的建议。 HAP 还可以指导房屋改造或修改建筑法规的工作。 “减少紧急情况下的死亡人数是一个很好的目标，但这是最低的目标，”气候研究员钱德尼·辛格说。

questions.text: According to Paragraph 1, Nagpur’s plan proposes measures to ____
One problem with existing HAPs is that they ____
options: prove too costly to be implemented.
give delayed responses to heat waves.

questions.text: Mumbai’s case shows that India’s heat alert systems need to ____
options: take subtle weather changes into account.
draw further support from local authorities.

questions.text: Kotharkar holds that a vulnerability map can help ____
options: prevent the harm of high humidity.
make relief plans for heat-stricken people.

questions.text: According to the last paragraph, researchers believe that HAPs should ____
options: serve a broader range of purposes.

paragraphs.en: Navigating beyond the organised pavements and parks of our urban spaces, desire paths are the unofficial footprints of a community, revealing the unspoken preferences, shared shortcuts and collective choices of humans. Often appearing as trodden dirt tracks through otherwise neat green spaces, these routes of collective disobedience cut corners, bisect lawns and cross hills, representing the natural capability of people (and animals) to go from point A to point B most effectively.
paragraphs.zh: 欲望之路超越了我们城市空间的有组织的人行道和公园，是一个社区的非官方足迹，揭示了人类不言而喻的偏好、共享的捷径和集体选择。这些集体抗命路线通常表现为穿过原本整洁的绿地的土路，它们抄近路、一分为二、穿过山丘，代表了人（和动物）最有效地从 A 点到 B 点的自然能力。

paragraphs.en: Urban planners interpret desire paths as more than just convenient shortcuts; they offer valuable insights into the dynamics between planning and behaviour. Ohio State University allowed its students to navigate the Oval, a lawn in the centre of campus, freely, then proceeded to pave the desire paths, creating a web of effective routes students had established.
paragraphs.zh: 城市规划者将欲望路径解释为不仅仅是方便的捷径；它们为规划和行为之间的动态提供了宝贵的见解。俄亥俄州立大学允许学生自由地在校园中心的草坪——椭圆形草坪上行走，然后开始铺设理想的道路，创建学生们建立的有效路线网。

paragraphs.en: Yet, reluctance persists among other planners to integrate desire paths into formal plans, citing concerns about safety, environmental impact, or primarily, aesthetics. A Reddit webpage devoted to the phenomenon, boasting nearly 50,000 members, showcases images of local desire paths adorned with signs instructing pedestrians to adhere to designated walkways, underscoring the rebellious nature inherent in these human-made tracks. This clash highlights an ongoing struggle between the organic, user-driven evolution of public spaces and the desire for a visually curated and controlled urban environment.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，其他规划者仍然不愿将愿望路径纳入正式规划，理由是担心安全、环境影响或主要是美观。 Reddit 的一个致力于这一现象的网页拥有近 5 万名会员，展示了当地欲望之路的图像，上面装饰着指示行人遵守指定人行道的标志，强调了这些人造轨道固有的叛逆本质。这种冲突凸显了公共空间的有机、用户驱动的演变与对视觉策划和控制的城市环境的渴望之间持续存在的斗争。

paragraphs.en: The Wickquasgeck Trail is an example of a historical desire path, created by Native Americans to cross the forests of Manhattan and move between settlements quickly. This trail, when Dutch colonists arrived, was widened and made into one of the main trade roads across the island, known at the time as de Heere Straat, or Gentlemen’s Street. Following the British assumption of control in New York, the street was renamed Broadway. Notably, Broadway stands out as one of the few areas in NYC that defies the grid-based system applied to the rest of the city, cutting a diagonal across parts of the city.
paragraphs.zh: 威克夸斯盖克步道 (Wickquasgeck Trail) 是历史上渴望之路的一个例子，由美洲原住民创建，用于穿越曼哈顿的森林并在定居点之间快速移动。当荷兰殖民者到达时，这条小路被拓宽，成为横贯该岛的主要贸易道路之一，当时被称为德希尔海峡或绅士街。英国控制纽约后，这条街更名为百老汇。值得注意的是，百老汇是纽约市少数几个不遵守应用于城市其他地区的基于网格的系统的地区之一，在城市的部分地区划出了一条对角线。

paragraphs.en: In online spaces, desire paths have sparked a fascination that can approach obsession, with the Reddit page serving as a hub. Contributors offer a wide array of stories, from little- known new shortcuts to long-established alternate routes.
paragraphs.zh: 在网络空间中，以 Reddit 页面为中心，欲望路径引发了一种近乎痴迷的迷恋。贡献者提供了各种各样的故事，从鲜为人知的新捷径到历史悠久的替代路线。

paragraphs.en: Animal desire paths, such as ducks forging trails through frozen ponds or dogs carving direct routes in gardens, highlight the adaptability of these trails in both human and animal experiences. As desire paths criss-cross through both physical and virtual landscapes, they stand as a proof of the collective insistence on forging unconventional routes and embracing the spirit of communal choice.
paragraphs.zh: 动物渴望的路径，例如鸭子在结冰的池塘中开辟出的路径或狗在花园中开辟出的直接路径，突出了这些路径在人类和动物体验中的适应性。当欲望路径在物理和虚拟景观中纵横交错时，它们证明了集体坚持开辟非传统路线和拥抱公共选择精神。

questions.text: According to Paragraph 1, desire paths are a result of ____
options: the curiosity to explore surrounding hills.
the necessity to preserve green spaces.
the wish to find comfort in solitude.

questions.text: It can be inferred that Ohio State University ____
options: intends to improve its desire paths.
leads in the research on desire paths.
guides the creation of its desire paths.
takes a positive view of desire paths.

questions.text: The images on the Reddit webpage reflect ____
options: conflicting opinions on the use of desire paths.
the call to upgrade the designing of public spaces.
the demand for proper planning of desire paths.
growing concerns over the loss of public spaces.

questions.text: The example of the Wickguasgeck Trail illustrates ____
options: the growth of New York City.
the Dutch origin of desire paths.

questions.text: It can be learned from the last paragraph that desire paths ____
options: reveal humans’ deep respect for nature.
are crucial to humans’ mental wellbeing.
are a human imitation of animal behavior.
show a shared trait in humans and animals.
